name: Desde Lietor a Ayna — circular en BTT
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Liétor are set within the Sierra del Segura mountains, offering a diverse landscape for outdoor activities. The region is characterized by dramatic valleys, gentle hills, and deep river gorges, with the village itself perched above the Mundo River. Lush pine forests, junipers, and holm oaks cover the terrain, providing varied environments for riding. This combination of mountainous and riverine features creates opportunities for challenging climbs, exhilarating descents, and technical sections through rocky and wooded areas. Best mountain bike trails around Liétor The most popular mountain bike trail is Sendas del río Segura — circular desde Elche de la Sierra, a 20.6 miles (33.1 km) moderate trail that takes about 3 hours 26 minutes to complete. This route explores the scenic river trails and varied terrain of the Sierra del Segura. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Letur Waterfall - circular from Elche de la Sierra, a difficult 31.3 miles (50.3 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and technical sections through forested areas and near natural features like waterfalls. Local mountain bikers also love the Desde Lietor a Ayna — circular en BTT, a 24.3 miles (39.2 km) trail leading through valleys and hills, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Mountain biking around Liétor is defined by river gorges, pine forests, and numerous viewpoints overlooking the valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Liétor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Liétor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Liétor?
A: Liétor offers nearly 20 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. The region's diverse landscape, with its river gorges, pine forests, and numerous viewpoints, provides a rich variety of routes for exploration.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Liétor?
A: While many routes in Liétor feature challenging climbs and technical sections, there are moderate options suitable for families with some mountain biking experience. These trails often follow river paths or gentle hills, allowing for an enjoyable outdoor adventure. Always check the route details for specific difficulty ratings.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Liétor?
A: You can expect a varied terrain in Liétor. Trails wind through dramatic valleys, gentle hills, and deep river gorges. The routes often feature lush pine forests, junipers, and holm oaks, with sections that can be rocky and technical, especially on more difficult paths. Some routes also include unpaved segments, adding to the authentic mountain biking experience.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes starting from Liétor?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. For example, the moderate route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1223190022/desde-lietor-a-ayna-circular-en-btt" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desde Lietor a Ayna — circular en BTT&lt;/a&gt; is a 39.2 km trail leading through valleys and hills, typically completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Another option is the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31459347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Liétor Old Town – El Pilar Fountain loop from Liétor&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 32.3 km.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, the region features natural attractions like waterfalls. The difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176823702/letur-waterfall-circular-from-elche-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Letur Waterfall - circular from Elche de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt; is a 50.3 km path that includes significant elevation changes and technical sections through forested areas and near natural features like waterfalls.

Q: What are the best trails for experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge?
A: Experienced mountain bikers will find several challenging routes in Liétor. The difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Infiernos Viewpoint – Aýna loop from Aýna&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 46.7 km with over 1,190 meters of elevation gain, offers a demanding ride with rewarding views. These routes often feature technical sections and significant climbs.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints accessible by mountain bike in the Liétor area?
A: Liétor is known for its breathtaking viewpoints. While cycling, you might encounter vistas such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5812222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Infiernos Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering incredible views overlooking the valley and the Mundo River, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5621407" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amílcar Barca Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, a natural viewpoint atop a cliff. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38169044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Infiernos Viewpoint – Aýna loop from Aýna&lt;/a&gt; is a specific route that incorporates one of these stunning locations.

Q: Are there any historical sites or interesting places to explore near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region has several points of interest. You can explore the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5706590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Liétor Old Town&lt;/a&gt;, which is spectacularly perched above the Mundo River gorge. The nearby settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5596072" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aýna&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable highlight in the area, often included in longer routes.

Q: What is the most popular mountain bike trail in Liétor?
A: The most popular mountain bike trail in the area is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176794272/sendas-del-rio-segura-circular-desde-elche-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sendas del río Segura — circular desde Elche de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 33.1 km route takes about 3 hours 26 minutes to complete and explores the scenic river trails and varied terrain of the Sierra del Segura.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Liétor?
A: The mountain bike routes in Liétor are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Over 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the unparalleled landscapes, hidden valleys, and challenging mountains that allow them to immerse themselves in the Mediterranean beauty of the region.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on mountain bike trails in Liétor?
A: The elevation gain varies significantly depending on the trail's difficulty. Moderate routes might feature around 800-900 meters of ascent, while more difficult trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176823702/letur-waterfall-circular-from-elche-de-la-sierra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Letur Waterfall - circular from Elche de la Sierra&lt;/a&gt;, can involve over 1,000 meters of climbing, providing a substantial workout amidst the mountainous landscape.

Q: Are there any specific trails that follow the Mundo River?
A: Many trails in the Liétor area are influenced by the Mundo River, given the village's spectacular perch above its gorge. While specific routes might not exclusively follow the river's edge for their entire length, you'll find that the river and its crystalline waters are a prominent feature of the landscape, creating picturesque spots ideal for rest and scenic riding.
tips: A charming town located in a mountainous landscape surrounded by hills and valleys. It has a long history dating back to Roman times, and has been inhabited by different cultures over the centuries. It is also known for its natural beauty, with the Mundo river and the Calares del Mundo y de la Sima natural park located close to the town.

A balcony-viewpoint located at a higher level than the road (you have to go up a staircase), overlooking the valley on a large rock protected by a stone wall and a metal fence. It has an information panel. Spectacular views of Aýna and the mountains.
